name: Modbus Server
description: The `modbus_server` component creates a RS485 connection to let your ESPHome node act as a Modbus server, allowing a ModBUS client to read data (like sensor values) from your ESPHome node.

config_entries.label: Address
config_entries.description: The Modbus address of the server device.

config_entries.label: Modbus ID
config_entries.description: Manually specify the ID of the `modbus` hub.

config_entries.label: Courtesy Response
config_entries.description: Configuration block to enable the courtesy response feature when the device is acting as a Modbus server.

config_entries.label: Enabled
config_entries.description: Whether to enable the courtesy response feature. Defaults to `false`.

config_entries.label: Register Last Address
config_entries.description: The highest Modbus register address (inclusive) up to which undefined registers are allowed to be read and will be padded with a default value. Any read request that includes undefined registers within this range will return the value specified by `register_value` instead of triggering an exception. Defaults to `65535`

config_entries.label: Register Value
config_entries.description: The 16-bit value (range: 0–65535) to return for undefined registers within the address range defined by `register_last_address`. Defaults to `0`.

config_entries.label: Registers
config_entries.description: A list of registers that are responded to when acting as a server.

config_entries.label: Address
config_entries.description: start address of the first register in a range

config_entries.label: Allow Partial Read
config_entries.description: For a multi-register value type (`U_DWORD`, `S_QWORD`, `FP32`, …), allow a read request to cover only part of the value — starting inside it or stopping short of its end — and return just the requested registers. When `false` the value is treated as atomic and any read that does not cover the whole value is rejected with an `ILLEGAL_DATA_ADDRESS` exception. Has no effect on single-register types (`U_WORD`/`S_WORD`). Defaults to `false`. > [!NOTE] > Even with `allow_partial_read` enabled, the `read_lambda` is still > evaluated **once per request**, so the registers returned within a single > request are always consistent with each other. A client that reads the > parts of a value in *separate* requests may observe a value that changed > in between — this is inherent to register-by-register Modbus access. > > This option affects reads only. Writes are always atomic: a write must > cover a whole value, since a partial value cannot be reconstructed for the > `write_lambda`.

config_entries.label: Read Lambda
config_entries.description: Lambda that returns the value of this register.

config_entries.label: Value Type
config_entries.description: datatype of the mod_bus register data. The default data type for ModBUS is a 16 bit integer in **big endian** format (network byte order, MSB first)

config_entries.label: Write Lambda
config_entries.description: Lambda that sets the value of this register. A variable `x` of the appropriate type (`uint16_t`, `int32_t`, etc, see above) is provided with the value, as well as `address` containing the address of this register. You must return `true` if the operation was successful, `false` otherwise, in which case a ModBUS exception code `4` will be sent to the client.
